title: T-003 — Yield + Factory KPI materialized views DDL (mv_yield_by_line_week, mv_yield_by_sku_week, mv_factory_kpi_week) (12-REPORTING §9.1)
prompt:
# T-003 — Yield + Factory KPI MV base schema

PRD: docs/prd/12-REPORTING-PRD.md §9.1, §5 Techniczne (RLS na MV constraint), §6 D-RPT-1 (MV vs real-time), §6 D-RPT-3 (weighted avg by kg_usage)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Create the 3 materialized views supporting Factory Overview, Yield by Line, Yield by SKU dashboards. RLS-via-service-layer pattern (Postgres MVs cannot enforce RLS natively; enforced through `app.current_org_id()` filtering at the data-access layer + REC-L1 `site_id` filtering). Use weighted average (kg_usage) per D-RPT-3.

## Implementation contract
1. Create migration `packages/db/migrations/0080_reporting_mv_yield.sql`:
   - `mv_yield_by_line_week (org_id, site_id, line_id, week_ending, kg_output, kg_usage, yield_pct, target_yield_pct, variance_pct, variance_gbp)` — selects from 08-PROD `wo_outputs` + `wo_consumptions` aggregated per line per ISO-Saturday week.
   - `mv_yield_by_sku_week (org_id, site_id, line_id, product_id, fg_code, week_ending, kg_output, yield_pct, target_yield_pct, variance_gbp)`.
   - `mv_factory_kpi_week (org_id, site_id, week_ending, weighted_yield_pct, weighted_ga_pct, avg_efficiency_pct, total_cases, total_variance_gbp)` — aggregates from mv_yield_by_line_week.
   - Unique indexes on (org_id, site_id, line_id, week_ending) etc. so `REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY` works.
   - REVOKE ALL on each MV from PUBLIC and `app_user`; GRANT SELECT to `app_reporting_role` only.
2. Drizzle schema entries in `packages/db/src/schema/reporting-views.ts` declaring read-only `pgMaterializedView` definitions for type-safety in queries.
3. Service-layer query helper `packages/reporting/src/queries/yield.ts` that always appends `WHERE org_id = app.current_org_id() AND (site_id = $current_site OR site_id IS NULL)` — RLS-via-service per §5 + REC-L1.
4. Vitest contract test asserts: (a) MV exists, (b) unique-index exists (refresh-concurrently prerequisite), (c) service helper always emits org_id+site_id filter (snapshot SQL string), (d) yield_pct math is kg_output / kg_usage (not unweighted) when seeded with synthetic 08-PROD rows, (e) cross-org leak test — query under org_A session never returns org_B rows.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0080_reporting_mv_yield.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/reporting-views.ts [create]
- packages/reporting/src/queries/yield.ts [create]
- packages/reporting/__tests__/mv-yield.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration 0080 runs against fresh DB seeded with 08-PROD wo_outputs/wo_consumptions, when `\d mv_yield_by_line_week` is inspected, then all declared columns + unique index `idx_mv_yield_line_week_pk` are present.
2. Given two orgs have seeded WO output rows, when `select * from mv_yield_by_line_week` runs via the service helper under org_A `app.set_org_context(...)` session, then only org_A rows return; cross_tenant_leak_test passes.
3. Given a line had kg_output=950 and kg_usage=1000, when mv_yield_by_line_week is refreshed, then yield_pct=95.0 (kg_output/kg_usage*100) — verified via vitest.
4. Given mv_factory_kpi_week.weighted_yield_pct is checked, when computed across two lines (line A kg_usage=1000 yield=95%, line B kg_usage=500 yield=80%), then result is (1000*95 + 500*80) / 1500 = 90.0 — weighted by kg_usage per D-RPT-3.
5. Given the service helper is called without an active org context, when invoked, then it throws `MissingOrgContextError` BEFORE issuing SQL (RLS-via-service guardrail).
6. Given the migration is inspected, when checked, then REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY succeeds (unique index exists on each MV).

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: `packages/reporting/__tests__/mv-yield.test.ts` mounts testcontainers Postgres, seeds 08-PROD producers, sets org context per session, asserts 6 ACs (incl. cross-org leak test + weighted-avg math + service helper guardrail).
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/reporting test mv-yield`.
- GREEN: write migration + Drizzle entries + service helper.

## Risk red lines
- Do not attempt RLS policies on materialized views — Postgres does not support; enforce via service-layer `where org_id = app.current_org_id()` instead (§5 Techniczne).
- Do not skip the unique index on each MV — required by `REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY` (T-005 cron).
- Do not use simple `AVG(yield_pct)` for factory KPI — must be weighted by kg_usage per D-RPT-3.
- Do not let MVs reference raw transactional joins inside an interactive request path; only the refresh cron touches them. Read-models only.
- Do not GRANT SELECT to PUBLIC; only `app_reporting_role` per security model.
- Do not embed real-time queries against `wo_outputs` in the dashboard service path; MV refresh handles cadence (D-RPT-1).

pipeline_inputs.description: Yield by line + Yield by SKU + Factory KPI materialized views with unique indexes (REFRESH CONCURRENTLY prerequisite) and service-layer RLS enforcement.
pipeline_inputs.details: Three MVs aggregate 08-PROD wo_outputs + wo_consumptions per ISO Saturday week. RLS-via-service-layer because Postgres MVs cannot host policies. Weighted-by-kg_usage average per D-RPT-3. Unique index per MV so REFRESH CONCURRENTLY works. REVOKE PUBLIC + GRANT only to app_reporting_role. REC-L1 site_id NULLable + filter `WHERE site_id = :current OR site_id IS NULL`.
out_of_scope: Do not implement cron refresh here (T-005)
Do not implement OEE consumer here (T-011)
Do not implement integration_health MV here (T-014)
cross_module_dependencies: 00-FOUNDATION T-040 R13 audit cols (audit log compatibility)
acceptance_criteria: Given migration 0080 runs against fresh DB seeded with 08-PROD wo_outputs/wo_consumptions, when `\d mv_yield_by_line_week` is inspected, then all declared columns + unique index idx_mv_yield_line_week_pk are present
Given a line had kg_output=950 and kg_usage=1000, when mv_yield_by_line_week is refreshed, then yield_pct=95.0 (kg_output/kg_usage*100)
Given two orgs have seeded WO output rows, when select runs via the service helper under org_A app.set_org_context session, then only org_A rows return; cross_tenant_leak_test passes
Given edge and compliance cases are exercised, when each condition is triggered, then it throws MissingOrgContextError before any SQL and result is 90.0 weighted by kg_usage per D-RPT-3.

GREEN: write migration + Drizzle entries + service helper
risk_red_lines: Do not attempt RLS policies on materialized views — Postgres does not support; enforce service-layer where org_id = app.current_org_id() (§5 Techniczne)
Do not skip unique index on each MV — required by REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY
Do not use simple AVG(yield_pct) for factory KPI — must be weighted by kg_usage (D-RPT-3)
Do not query raw transactional joins on the dashboard hot path (D-RPT-1) — read-model views only
Do not GRANT SELECT to PUBLIC; only app_reporting_role
Do not bypass app.current_org_id() with current_setting GUC reads
